title: Corybas mirabilis
taxonomy: Corybas mirabilis is a species of orchid in the genus Corybas, which belongs to the subfamily Diurideae. This subfamily is part of the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. The family Orchidaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, with over 30,000 known species worldwide. Corybas mirabilis is native to Australia and is known for its unique and intricate flowers. The genus Corybas is thought to have evolved over 20 million years ago, with the species mirabilis being a part of this ancient lineage.

intro: Corybas mirabilis, also known as the mirror orchid, is a rare and unusual species of orchid that belongs to the genus Corybas, characterized by its unique, mirror-like appearance due to the reflection of light on the surface of its leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, making it a valuable resource for treating various ailments such as skin infections and respiratory issues.
